Patch Set 1:

Looks like a bunch of sanity test failures related to this patch?

I was also going to say that this should get a test case to confirm that the layout is not copied onto the subdir.

I wonder if this needs LU-11656 to also be fixed for the tests to pass?

Patch Set 1:

Hi Andreas,

I can not reproduce the failures locally on one node and need to investigate further.

Patch Set 2:

More debug logs showed that lds->lds_dir_def_striping_set was true in lod_ah_init():

 (lod_object.c:4746:lod_ah_init()) Process entered
 (osd_handler.c:314:osd_idc_find_and_init()) lustre-MDT0000: FID [0x200000007:0x1:0x0] not in the id map cache
 (lod_object.c:4787:lod_ah_init()) lds->lds_dir_def_striping_set is true
 (lod_object.c:4679:lod_striping_from_default()) lds->lds_dir_def_striping_set is true
 (lod_object.c:4692:lod_striping_from_default()) striping from default dir: count:2, offset:1, hash_type:1
 (lod_object.c:4829:lod_ah_init()) final dir stripe [2 1 1]
 (lod_object.c:4831:lod_ah_init()) Process leaving

However, the only place that sets lds->lds_dir_def_striping_set to 1 is in lod_get_default_lmv_striping(), which was _not_ called in the code path.

In addition, lds is originally specified as "lds = &info->lti_def_striping", however, after reviewing the codes, I didn't find a place that the lds_dir_def_striping_set flag in lti_def_striping was set to 1.

So, the next step is to find out where lds_dir_def_striping_set was set to 1.

Patch Set 2:

It turns out in the original lod_ah_init() codes, after specifying "lds = &info->lti_def_striping", the structure was not initialized and contained arbitrary data. I'm updating the patch to fix this.

Patch Set 2:

Code-wise looks good to me.

Patch Set 5:

(1 comment)

Naming concern aside, this looks good.

Patch Set 5: Code-Review-1

(1 comment)

Also, the lfs-setstripe.1 man page needs to be updated to reflect that directories created under root will not explicitly copy the default layout at creation time, but will implicitly inherit the default layout at runtime.

Patch Set 5:

(1 comment)

Andreas,

It's not obvious to me what inherited at runtime means...

What will happen to a file created in a directory in the root?  What about a directory under that, then a file created in that subdir?

Patch Set 5:

> It's not obvious to me what inherited at runtime means...
 >
 > What will happen to a file created in a directory in the root? 
 > What about a directory under that, then a file created in that
 > subdir?

All files created in the filesystem will use the filesystem-wide default layout, as set on the root directory of the filesystem, unless the file is explicitly created with a different layout using "lfs setstripe" (or API equivalent), or it is created in a subdirectory which specifies a default layout different from that of the root directory.

Patch Set 5:

Ah, I understand now - The default on the root *is* the global default.  It's just *also* inherited in to the xattrs of subdirectories, and we (understandably) want to change that.  This all makes sense now - thank you.
Uploaded patch set 6.

parents.subject: LU-930 doc: move server man pages to server package

commit.subject: LU-11739 lod: don't inherit default layout from root directory
commit.message:
LU-11739 lod: don't inherit default layout from root directory

There is no need to inherit the default directory layout from
the root directory when subdirectories are created therein.
This consumes xattr space on the subdirectories, and makes it
more complex to change the filesystem default layout in the future.

This patch fixes the above issue in lod_ah_init() to check if
the parent directory is the root directory and not copy
the default layout xattr to the new subdirectory.
